def: A coming or bringing together, as in a public meeting, in adispute, in the act of comparing, or in sexual intercourse. [R.] Jer.Taylor.
A division of acalephs or jellyfishes, including most of thelarge disklike species.-- Dis*coph"o*rous, a.
To deprive of a commission or trust. [R.] Laud.
One of a special breed of hounds used for chasing foxes.
A mock serenade of discordant noises, made with kettles, tinhorns, etc., designed to annoy and insult.
The act or process of renovating; the state of being renovatedor renewed. Thomson.There is something inexpressibly pleasing in the annual renovation ofthe world. Rabbler.
Divination by dropping melted wax in water.
To cut off; to separate or expel from union; to extirpate.Barrow.The second presbytery of Philadelphia was also exscinded by thatAssembly. Am. Cyc.
A name applied by Dujardin in 1835 to the gelatinous materialforming the bodies of the lowest animals; protoplasm.
